title: How to Do Laundry with Baking Soda and Vinegar: A Step-by-Step Guide
author: Bulkley Laundry
excerpt: We understand that doing laundry is a chore that most people dread, but it is a necessary task that needs to be done. Many people …
content:
We understand that doing laundry is a chore that most people dread, but it is a necessary task that needs to be done. Many people wonder if there is a way to make doing laundry easier, and we are here to tell you that there is. In this article, we will show you how to do laundry using baking soda and vinegar.

Why Use Baking Soda and Vinegar?

Baking soda and vinegar are two of the most versatile household products that you can use for cleaning. They are also very affordable and eco-friendly. Baking soda is a natural deodorizer and stain remover that can help to neutralize odors and remove tough stains. Vinegar is a natural fabric softener that can help to remove soap residue and static from your clothes. When used together, they can help to make your clothes cleaner and fresher than ever before.

Step-by-Step Guide to Doing Laundry with Baking Soda and Vinegar

  1. Sort Your Clothes The first step to doing laundry is to sort your clothes by color and fabric type. Separate your whites, colors, and delicates to prevent any bleeding or damage to your clothes.
  2. Pre-Treat Stains Before you start your laundry, pre-treat any stains on your clothes with a baking soda and water paste. Mix ¼ cup of baking soda with 1 tablespoon of water to form a thick paste. Apply the paste to the stain and let it sit for 15-30 minutes before washing.
  3. Add Baking Soda to the Wash Add ½ cup of baking soda to your washing machine before adding your clothes. Baking soda can help to neutralize odors and remove tough stains from your clothes.
  4. Use Vinegar as a Fabric Softener Instead of using a commercial fabric softener, add ½ cup of vinegar to your washing machine during the rinse cycle. Vinegar can help to remove soap residue and static from your clothes, leaving them feeling soft and fresh.
  5. Dry Your Clothes After your clothes have been washed, remove them from the washing machine and either hang them up to dry or place them in the dryer. If you want to save energy and money, hang your clothes up to dry instead of using the dryer.

Conclusion

Doing laundry can be a daunting task, but it doesn’t have to be. By using baking soda and vinegar, you can make your clothes cleaner and fresher than ever before. Follow the steps above to do your laundry with baking soda and vinegar and enjoy the benefits of a more eco-friendly and affordable laundry routine.
